summary: Replaced the first-screen bottom/peripheral information wall with compact task rail cards and one active collapsed drawer, keeping route/appointment/campaign actions on existing read-model and command/query boundaries.
commit: 71d23b5d; no commit made, review the working tree diff
requested_action: Review the task rail/collapsed drawer UX recovery implementation against M7-TASK-RAIL-COLLAPSED-DRAWERS-001 acceptance criteria.

tests_run.result: Working tree has modified task files only: .storybook/WebClientShell.stories.tsx, apps/web/src/styles/shell.css, packages/ui/src/i18n.ts, packages/ui/src/index.tsx, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts, tests/ui-m2-district-list.test.tsx. No commit made.
Task list loaded; M7-TASK-RAIL-COLLAPSED-DRAWERS-001 is IN_PROGRESS for client_engineer.
Command completed with no ready-task output.
TASK_JSON_SHAPE_OK M7-TASK-RAIL-COLLAPSED-DRAWERS-001 IN_PROGRESS using the actual task schema: id, status, owner_role, reviewer_role, risk_level, scope.allowed_paths, scope.forbidden_paths.
Vite production build passed. Build reported existing chunk-size warnings for lib/main chunks over 500 kB.
Playwright passed: 23/23 tests. Earlier e2e run failed 4 tests while old default-expanded assumptions were still present; tests and layout were corrected before the passing rerun.
Passed format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check. Earlier local retries caught formatting, one unused prop, and one helper type issue; all were fixed before the passing run.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Bottom panels default to compact task rail cards for obligations, appointments, succession, campaign prep, notifications, and results.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: TaskRail renders six cards with data-task-rail-card-kind for obligations, appointments, succession, campaign, notifications, and results; unit/e2e assertions cover card count and card kinds.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Only the currently relevant drawer expands; other areas preserve map context and do not swamp the first screen.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: ClientShellView tracks activeTaskDrawer, defaults to obligations, and renders one Expanded task drawer. Appointment and guidance flows are reachable by selecting their cards instead of defaulting visible.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Task rail cards state one problem, one reason, one action, and severity/urgency using text, icon/shape, and color.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: ClientTaskRailCard includes problem, reason, actionLabel, urgency, severity, and icon; CSS applies severity-specific color treatment and fixed icon cells. Text is localized through packages/ui/src/i18n.ts.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.criterion: Responsive coverage includes 1280x720 and 1440x900 without text overlap or hidden primary actions.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.evidence: Playwright e2e passed required viewport overflow coverage, including 1280x720 and 1440/desktop checks. Drawer-specific route queue and appointment flow CSS keeps overflow local and primary actions visible.

decisions: Kept React as DOM UI owner and consumed existing read-model slices; no WorldState mutation or sim/protocol changes were made.
Moved the always-visible appointment flow, guidance notes, route queue, campaign prep, notifications, and results behind compact task rail cards with one expanded drawer.
Kept the route queue sortable and virtualized in the obligations drawer, using local drawer overflow instead of page-level overflow.
Added Storybook states for normal, empty, error, extreme, and narrow task rail coverage.
Kept all new player-visible strings in the existing en-US and zh-CN i18n catalogs.
risks: QA should perform visual review of the compact rail at 1280x720, 1440x900, high-DPI, and text scaling because automated checks prove no overflow but not final player comprehension.
The obligations drawer preserves the full sortable route table with local horizontal overflow in narrow rail space; this is functional but may need UX review for ideal scanning density.
Vite build still reports chunk-size warnings over 500 kB; this task did not address bundling.
M7 content lock remains not accepted; manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE.
